Connection.title: vsCRT: Connect All Servers in Folder

Credentials & crypto.title: vsCRT: Remove Host Key from known_hosts

settings.description: Where the SSH terminal opens when you double-click a server in the Connection view. Overridden by a node's 'terminalLocation' field if set.
enumDescriptions: Bottom terminal panel (split window)
Editor area as a full tab

settings.description: Where the SSH terminal opens when you click the inline row-action button on a server (the one that appears on hover), invoke Connect from a context menu item, or use the command palette. Overridden by a node's 'terminalLocation' field if set.
enumDescriptions: Bottom terminal panel (split window)
Editor area as a full tab

settings.description: How vsCRT treats a host whose key isn't in ~/.ssh/known_hosts. ProxyJump chains fall back to OpenSSH's interactive in-terminal prompt when this is set to 'prompt-on-first'.
enumDescriptions: Trust on first use (OpenSSH accept-new). Unknown hosts are added silently.
Prompt with a modal fingerprint dialog before adding an unknown host to known_hosts. Subsequent connections are strict.
Refuse any unknown host. You must add keys to ~/.ssh/known_hosts outside vsCRT.

settings.description: Minimum passphrase strength score (0=very weak … 4=very strong) accepted when setting or changing the vault passphrase.
enumDescriptions: Accept any passphrase (only minimum-length enforced).
Reject very weak passphrases.
Reject weak passphrases.
Require strong passphrases (recommended default).
Require very strong passphrases (highest entropy tier).

settings.description: Controls whether vsCRT writes per-session recordings to ~/.vscrt/sessions/. Off by default; minimal records structured metadata only; full additionally captures terminal output.
enumDescriptions: Disable session recording entirely.
Record session metadata only (server, auth mode, timestamps). No transcript is written.
Record metadata PLUS the full terminal transcript to disk. WARNING: transcripts may contain passwords, tokens, or other sensitive output — enable only on trusted machines.

settings.description: Controls what vsCRT writes to ~/.vscrt/connections.log on each connect attempt. The log rolls at 5 MB to a .1 backup; it is local-only — nothing ever leaves the machine.
enumDescriptions: Disable the connection log entirely. Nothing is written to ~/.vscrt/connections.log.
Record timestamp, server name, auth mode, and outcome. Endpoints and error messages are NOT persisted.
Record everything from 'minimal' plus the full endpoint (user@host:port) and error message text.

settings.description: Optional list of additional vsCRT config files to load as READ-ONLY overlays. Each file is merged into the tree under a synthetic 'Shared (read-only)' folder. Password fields are stripped; only publickey / agent auth flows through. Requires workspace trust. Paths support '~' expansion; combine with ${workspaceFolder} for repo-scoped configs.
When to clear the cached Argon2id-derived key. The passphrase itself is always re-prompted on next use after lock.
enumDescriptions: Never auto-lock. The Argon2id-derived key stays cached until manual lock or VS Code quits.
Auto-lock after 5 minutes of passphrase inactivity.
Auto-lock after 15 minutes of passphrase inactivity.
Auto-lock after 30 minutes of passphrase inactivity.
Auto-lock after 1 hour of passphrase inactivity.
Auto-lock whenever the VS Code window loses OS focus.

capabilities.notes: OS-keychain or Argon2id-encrypted-in-file; never on argv.
With explicit identityFile, or via ssh-agent when no file is pinned.
Per-node `commands` array — Run Command… in context menu.
vscrt-bundle/v1 with Argon2id key derivation or stripped audit mode.

Right-click a server → Open SFTP… for an interactive sftp terminal, or Open SFTP Browser (Preview)… for a read-only directory view with breadcrumbs and ls-style listings. Both share auth, ProxyJump, and host-key policy with the connect flow. For a full drag-and-drop uploader, pair with Remote-SSH.
On roadmap. Today vsCRT handles SSH only.

diagnose: Run `vsCRT: Test Connection` — classifies as connected / auth-failed / timeout / refused / no-route.
If the probe succeeds but the full connect hangs, check `vsCRT.hostKeyPolicy` — a modal fingerprint prompt may be blocked.
Temporarily add `-vvv` to `extraArgs` for verbose SSH debug output in the terminal.

troubleshooting.symptom: Host key has changed / MITM warning
diagnose: Right-click the server → `Remove Host Key…` to clear the stale entry.
Reconnect — the TOFU modal shows the new SHA-256 fingerprint for you to verify.
For stricter environments, set `vsCRT.hostKeyPolicy` to `strict` and manage known_hosts externally.
Confirm the identityFile path is the PRIVATE key (no `.pub`).
Status view shows whether ssh-agent is reachable — if so, leave `identityFile` blank to use agent keys.
Run `vsCRT: Generate SSH Key Pair` and use `Install Public Key Now` on the server form.
`vsCRT: Show Output Log` — look for Argon2id parameter mismatches.
If the params shifted after an upgrade, `vsCRT: Rotate Passphrase KDF Parameters` will re-key every blob.
Last resort: `vsCRT: Reset Passphrase Setup` — wipes the check token but leaves node ciphertexts; you'll re-enrol on next use.
`vsCRT: Validate Config` pinpoints the JSON error with a pointer path.
`vsCRT: Restore Config from Backup…` rolls back to the most recent auto-saved backup (cap: 10).
For schema drift: open `~/.vscrt/vscrtConfig.json` in the editor — VS Code surfaces squiggles against the published schema.
